results.rendered_html:
vidvas a. [vid-kvasu] (Nom. sing. m. vidvān; f. viduṣī; n. vidvat)
1
(pb) Knowing (with acc.); ānaṃdaṃ brahmaṇo vidvān na bibheti kadācana; tava vidvānapi tāpakāraṇaṃ  R. 8. 76 ;  Ki. 11. 30. 
2 Wise, learned.
— m. A learned or wise man, scholar; kiṃ vastu vidvan gurave pradeyaṃ  R. 5. 18. 
Comp.
— kalpa, -deśīya, -deśya a. (vidvatkalpa, vidvaddeśīya, vidvaddeśya) slightly learned, a little learned.
— janaḥ (vidvajjanaḥ) a learned or wise man, sage.
